 {"id":"wa-809y","title":"Workflow audit integration: record per-step actions/outcomes into audit trail","description":"# Task: Workflow audit integration

## Goal
Ensure workflows are explainable and trustworthy by recording an audit trail for every workflow action attempt.

Workflows can:
- send text
- issue control sequences (Ctrl-C)
- wait for conditions
- perform account/device-auth steps (later)

Users need to know exactly what wa did and why.

## Requirements
- For each workflow execution:
  - record an audit entry for each step action attempt (even if denied or failed)
  - include `step_id` / `step_name` / `workflow_name`
  - include policy decision and preconditions observed
  - include verification outcomes (wait-for matched, timeout)

- Redaction:
  - never store raw secrets or full inputs
  - store hashes/summaries consistent with the redaction system

- Correlation:
  - link audit entries to `workflow_execution_id`
  - link to the originating `event_id` when available

## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - run a synthetic workflow that attempts a send and a wait
  - verify audit timeline entries exist for both attempts and correlate to the workflow execution
- Redaction tests:
  - ensure the audit record never contains raw inputs/pane tails

## Acceptance Criteria
- A workflow run produces a coherent audit timeline that matches the workflow_step_log.
- Failures still produce audit entries that explain what was attempted and what was denied.

 {"id":"wa-86ov","title":"CLI: wa audit tail --follow","description":"## What
Add a CLI command to stream audit logs in JSONL.

## Why
Operators need real-time visibility and integrations.

## How
- `wa audit tail --follow` prints JSONL records
- Support `--since` and `--limit`

## Success Criteria
- Output is data-only (no ANSI)
- Streaming resumes without duplicates","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:04.676831223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.223558-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-04T07:03:25.473758755Z","close_reason":"Implemented wa audit tail JSONL streaming","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-86ov","depends_on_id":"wa-wwsx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-86ov","depends_on_id":"wa-kuy6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-86ov","depends_on_id":"wa-d8d1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-8bk","title":"[EPIC] UX Excellence: Explainability, Ergonomics, and User Delight","description":"# [EPIC] UX Excellence: Explainability, Ergonomics, and User Delight

## Mission
Transform wa from a capable but opaque automation tool into a **transparent, delightful, and trustworthy** system that users love to use.

## Why This Matters
Terminal automation is inherently scary. Users cannot see what wa is doing, and when things go wrong, they often do not understand why. This creates:
- **Fear**: "What if it sends to the wrong pane?"
- **Frustration**: "Why did it deny my send?"
- **Distrust**: "I do not know what it is doing"

Great UX transforms these into:
- **Confidence**: "I can preview before executing"
- **Understanding**: "I know exactly why it made that decision"
- **Trust**: "I can see everything happening in real-time"

## Core Insight: Legibility
The unifying theme across all these improvements is **legibility**—making wa's behavior visible and understandable:
1. **Legible decisions** → Deep Explainability (`wa why` + enriched errors)
2. **Legible state** → Interactive TUI Dashboard
3. **Legible intentions** → Dry-run preview mode
4. **Legible history** → Event timeline with correlation
5. **Legible next steps** → Quick-fix suggestions

## Scope (30 Improvements)
This epic encompasses improvements across multiple dimensions:

### Reliability & Robustness (5)
- Watchdog/heartbeat with auto-restart
- Circuit breaker for failed services
- Chaos testing harness
- Retry with exponential backoff
- Graceful degradation modes

### Performance (5)
- Lazy pattern compilation
- Bloom filter pre-filter
- Connection pooling for WezTerm CLI
- Background compression
- Incremental FTS sync

### User Experience & Ergonomics (5)
- Interactive TUI dashboard (expand existing)
- Shell completions for all commands
- Progressive disclosure in output
- Smart aliases
- Natural language event descriptions

### Intuitiveness & Discoverability (5)
- `wa why` command for decision explanation
- `wa learn` interactive tutorial
- Contextual help in every error
- Smart defaults based on environment detection
- In-CLI documentation

### Usefulness & Compelling Features (5)
- Session recording and replay
- Event timeline with cross-pane correlation
- Notification webhooks (Slack/Discord/email)
- Export to markdown/HTML
- Proactive suggestions ("Account X at 85%")

### Safety & Trust (5)
- Dry-run for all human commands
- Confirmation prompts with policy reason
- Rollback log visualization
- Simulation mode (mock WezTerm)
- Plugin/extension system for custom patterns

## Success Criteria
- New users can understand wa's behavior within 10 minutes
- Errors are self-explanatory and include actionable fixes
- Users can preview any action before executing
- The TUI provides real-time visibility into all operations
- Support burden decreases because users can self-serve

## Testing
Each sub-feature has its own testing requirements. At the epic level:
- User testing with 3+ external developers (collect feedback)
- Measure time-to-first-success for new users
- Track error resolution time (should decrease)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 30 improvement areas have implemented beads with tests
- Documentation covers all new commands and features
- TUI is polished and accessible (keyboard navigation, screen reader support)
- Error messages meet the "actionable fix included" standard

 {"id":"wa-upg.9.5","title":"Tests: triage scoring/prioritization + schema stability","description":"# Task: Tests for triage

## Goal
Lock in triage behavior and contract.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - scoring produces expected ordering for fixtures
- Output tests:
  - JSON schema stable
  - deterministic ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- Triage changes are intentional and visible via failing tests.

 {"id":"wa-upg.9.6","title":"E2E: triage with multiple simultaneous issues (artifacts + suggested actions)","description":"# Task: E2E triage multi-issue scenario

## Goal
Validate that triage prioritization works in realistic multi-symptom situations.

## Scenario
Create multiple issues:
- a recent crash bundle
- a repeated event with escalation
- a stuck workflow
- a health warning (index lag)

Run `wa triage` and assert ordering and suggested actions.

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - triage JSON output
  - logs for each simulated issue

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates operator usefulness (clear ordering and actions).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:48:10.142446513Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-31T02:00:19.746669839Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T02:00:19.746584831Z","close_reason":"Added triage_multi_issue E2E + triage health snapshot fallback"}
 {"id":"wa-upg.9.7","title":"Docs: operator playbook (using triage + why + reproduce)","description":"# Task: Document operator playbook

## Goal
Provide a pragmatic operator guide for keeping wa healthy.

## Requirements
- Document common flows:
  - triage → why → fix
  - triage → reproduce → file issue
  - triage → mute/noise control
- Provide copy-paste examples.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new user can recover from common incidents using only the playbook.

 {"id":"wa-uyve","title":"[EPIC] Saved searches + scheduled alerts","description":"## Background
Operators repeatedly run the same search/event filters to track specific agent states. Today this is manual and easy to forget.

## Goals
- Persist named searches (query + filters + scope) for reuse
- Allow scheduled runs that emit notifications when results appear
- Integrate with CLI/TUI/web dashboards without duplicating search logic

## Non-Goals
- New query language (reuse existing FTS syntax)
- External alert delivery (handled in notification channels epic)

## Considerations
- Saved search must be safe to execute (no side effects)
- Scheduling should be rate-limited and observable
- Outputs must be redacted per safety config

## Success Criteria
- Users can create/list/run/delete saved searches
- Scheduled alerts fire with clear context and can be disabled
- Full unit + e2e test coverage with verbose logs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:03.835297223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.253264-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:29.507396585Z","close_reason":"All child beads completed; closing stale-open epic to keep active plan set non-duplicative."}
 {"id":"wa-uz4s","title":"Fix broken HEAD: add search_explain module declaration","description":"HEAD was broken because main.rs referenced wa_core::search_explain but lib.rs lacked the pub mod declaration. Added module declaration, 3 storage methods needed by search_explain, and fixed clippy or_fun_call warning. Commit: 31a8697","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:58:38.98471368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.250906-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:58:48.108662635Z"}
 {"id":"wa-uzq7","title":"Docs: storage/index tuning + maintenance commands","description":"# Task: Document storage/indexing tuning

## Goal
Make storage behavior predictable and tunable for power users.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - how WAL/checkpointing works in wa
  - how to interpret index lag signals
  - recommended maintenance commands (explicit vacuum/optimize)
  - how to collect perf evidence for bug reports

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can self-diagnose slow search and provide actionable data in an issue.
